title_guess: Kent & France: Ightham Mote/Stalisfield, Loire (Beaugency, David at Treignac), Tournon/Tournus, St. Jean de Vaux, Canterbury & London

pages.text:
Sunday Simon + Eve picked up Tinder at 9.30 so only £15. Then we went to St. Fagan's museum. The main building opens later in the year. So we looked at out door buildings that have been moved there.
    A gorse mill - Small. 2 men + 1 boy wd. take 20 min. to process a bunch of gorse - This was fed to livestock. Cd. be fed all year + grew on worst soil, so better soil cd be used for other crops.
    We went to a pub for lunch + raced home (lots of traffic) + got there with 10 minutes to spare to get to church.
    __Thursday May 3rd__
    We set off at 9, voted (Newman House) for city councillors + dropped off Tinder at Kenads. Lunch in the van at Cobham services + on to Ightham Mote. Lovely [C4] house. Beautiful linen fold panelling, paint colour in rooms good, welcoming. Well worth a visit. V.beautiful.
    On to the Plough Inn Stalisfield, where Simon has already booked supper. They have put us in the fireplace again. They are building

captions: Ightham Mote — Kent 2018 — House, garden and estate (National Trust leaflet cover, glued in)
pages.text: holiday lets in the next field. It has been cold for days but now there is nice warm evening sunshine.

We got to the Tunnel an hour early & they put us onto the next train - ahead of the Bank Holiday weekend.
We drove past Etaples + Rouen + are camping beyond Dreux at a Municipal Park at St. Remysur l'Avre. The river splits in 2 in the town.
A lovely, au warm evening walk in the Sunshine. There's an Irishman in the camp who sits in his car all day.
Delicious
    eclairs
1/3 fields oil seed rape
[illegible] [illegible]

captions: Restaurant L'Atelier de Jeanne, 3 impasse du Chateau - Meung sur Loire
pages.text:
[?Bble] Saturday.
      We shopped in Dreux + lunched overlooking R. le [?lour] a few kilometres north of Chateaudun. We've camped at le [?Floe] at Beaugency. lots of watermills, broad river. Old bridges. Church has a capitol of David & Goliath & font from 1593. A good meal (-except pudding). A nice town to wander in.

      Lovely views from the van across the river.

__Sunday__ a near-perfect day so far.
Warm sunshine in the van first thing. An 11 mile bike ride to Meung. lovely bike track. Fields, villages.
Delicious lasagna, rose + lashings of chocolate on the profiteroles at this restaurant. Frogs making a tremendous racket.
A market in Meung + beatiful Romanesque door outside church + nice Stained glass window.
Back to Beaugency. The bridge is beautifal.
Both campsites have been only £11 per night.

__Monday__ We're off to see David. Helen + he have flown there. John + Kay should be there too in the Caterham.
Lots of mills at Beaugency – "In the Town? They 've routed the water, dividing the stream in 2 before it got to the [illegible] river the water was reused.

[photo caption: Le Rû]
[French text on photo: Au fil du Rû, long de 2700 mètres, onze moulins sont installés au Moyen Age, dans les quartiers du Prateau et des Marais. La rivière, de faible débit est aménagée afin d'assurer une énergie hydraulique suffisante. En complément, dans sa partie supérieure des moulins à vent sont construits au XVIII° siècle pour pallier le manque de rendement. Ils disparaissent au début du XX° siècle. Les moulins à eau sont devenus des résidences particulières et ne sont pas accessibles au public.]

We reached David a bit later than planned. It was lovely to have a bit of the house & garden.

__Tuesday__ We managed a 14½ mile walk to Treignac lake – gorgeous old roads + scenery. Yoga helps stop the stiffness. Nice to spend time with everyone. I had a ride in the Caterha[m]
__Wednesday__ John + Kay left after Simon had driven the Caterham. They will cross at St Malo tomorrow. David, Helen, Simon + [I] went to Treignac for the bank, shoes for Helen + seeds. The others went for a shorter walk after lunch. We all did some gardening. Nasturtiums. David has cooked delicious [illegible] stew. There are 2 beautiful matched white horses from the Camargue in David's field, for a couple of weeks, mowing the grass for him. Mr. Levine is anxious – the mare has a bad hoof. If he has to put her down (21 years old) the other will have to be put down too. They are inseperable.

Thursday May 10th.
We left at 9.30 & came south, as directed by David, through Meymac, Bort, Riom, Florenc. We are camping on R. Hepault (wine growing) near caves of the Madelons. We have passed over causse, very dry limestone, gorsey not farmland. Down here it's warmer & grapes are harvested.

Friday
[?Deny] RH died on Wed. Olivia Tufnoll died [strikethrough: Thu] Friday
We are in camping d'Anglas near Brossac.
A beautiful meadow with the sound of a river.
We saw those amazing caves - Grotte dos-demoisell[?es]
Millions of years old. Just after lunch at Ganges David Fellowes rang to say Ols has died (She had died just before noon.)
We returned to the camp via a supermarket.
Simon swam. We ride bikes to the shower house.

[?Thursday] May 12th.
We walked along paths to Brossac. The castle looks beautifully built. We walked around it & looked at the old town then went down to the new town. The river [strikethrough: is] bank is full of bamboo. There's an old house & paper factory guarded by an alsation. We had lunch at L'Arboussede. A local [strikethrough: the] petanque club was also having a lunch there. Good trout.

Back at the van we just got the floor swept before rain poured down.

Why are so many of the vines left unwedded?

__Sunday__
A difficult start to the day. My loo was overflowing, so I had to bail it out. We stopped to look at an old bridge ([?2]) & church - St. [?Catherine] Iss.... & Simon lost the lens in his glasses, & it poured & poured rain & we were cold & couldn't find it. He's now driving in dark glasses. Things got better when we

silk worms
eating
mulberry
leaves

got to the Silk Museum at St. Hyppolyte du Fauve. It was fascinating & very interesting. Dreadful conditions for women. At its height in 1860. Pasteur found a cure for the silk worm [strikethrough: disease] [?disease] (p? nosema). a [?feneges].

We had the heater on during lunch (7°) & are now going to a hotel for the night. We have now gone into a huge jam, 1½ hrs had supper & are now in slow-moving traffic to our hotel at Tournon. I ended up having to drive in the dark & rain because of Simon's glasses, which was pretty fraught. The hotel room was nice. It looked onto the Rhône. [strikethrough: The] The [strikethrough: two] town seems to be in a valley surrounded by vines...

captions: The university was founded by Cardinal Tourmon.
pages.text:
[postcard of Tournon-sur-Rhône glued in]

The university was founded by Cardinal Tourmon.

Monday

Having left the hotel, we motor-wayed to Lyons & spent an hour trying to find a parking place without Success. There's a rail strike & they've blocked the main square from parking. It would have been nice to have a meal.

We have come to a place the Same distance north of Lyons (as Tournon) we're now at a rainy

__Tournous__, an important Mediaeval Monastery town.

We had a delicious supper after looking around the abbey. Simon had cheese & salad & I had murdered eggs (vinegar.) The plates were really hot. Then Simon had steak & I had chicken. Then Simon had crème brûlée & I had chocolate mousse. A really good meal in our hotel de la Paix.
__Tuesday__ Overcast & damp, but cleared up by lunch time & warmer now at tea time. We saw the abbey again [?It] was the bishop's mark. St. Philibert is [?it] buried here. We saw the upper room with 3 huge organ bellows, the crypt & the cloister. Lovely. And a beautiful "Brown Madonna", where I lit a candle for Olivia.
We wandered around the town - beautiful stone carved doorways & gutters (all clean) down the middle of cobbled streets.
We're now at St. Jean de Vaux. The sun is shining, although there might be thunder.
__Wednesday__
There was lots of thunder in the night. But th[e] full [?in] day h[as] had OK weather. Sometim[es]

sunny. We have camped at [?Port mirail] in the municipal park. Near the sports field. V. few people about. The [?Accent] [?acceuil] woman gave us the name of a local restaurant that had closed 3 years previously. A nice has-been town. We ended up having a good meal at le Grand Condé. The camp site only charges [strikethrough]£[/strikethrough] € 5.5. We just missed a huge rainshower. At supper we sat next to a man [?mending] leather machines - Louis Vuitton
__Thursday__ Royal wedding for Prince Harry which we are going to miss. (Here I missed out that we camped nr. [?Chinee] country)
We went the wrong way north & ended up driving on a beautiful road, past [?Bonneil] village. I'd like to go back & walk there, near Châton-Thierry.
On to a large Auchan. Simon had trouble getting more camping gas because of a stupid cashier. However we reached an earlier Channel Train with time to spare. Men were using a drone in different places. [?Walking] something. What? Why? A man stood with cones in a circle round him on different sites.
    We reached Roksell Farm
& William joined us for lunch. We spent a

couple of hours looking at [?stuff] hop picking machines. We are buying stuff including a plucker bank for £10,000. A nice man, Anthony, 28 years old. Very self-assured. His first cousin has inherited [strikethrough]the[/strikethrough] half the grand-father's land (originally 3 or 400 acres) but doesn't work as hard. Anthony grows other things besides hops, including onions. He has an amazing new Wolf (double wolf machine) & was taking the Germans to the airport.
    We decided to come back to the Plough campsite again in Stalisfield. — A good evening. Another good supper.
__Friday__ Sunshine flooded the camping van at breakfast. Off to Canterbury. 597 Augustine arrived. His tome was here for a while. 1170 Thomas à Becket murdered. Lovely window of Adam Delving. We liked the undercroft. Gormley [?floats] body sculpture of nails. Great Norman pillars & capitols. 2 columns from 601. Chapter house with good ceiling & 1896 windows.
    People were friendly & bus driver told us a better place for the van Park & Ride.

captions: [?62] murals St Gabriel's Chapel, Crypt, Canterbury Cathedral
pages.text:
[glued-in postcard photograph of mural] [?62] murals St Gabriel's Chapel, Crypt, Canterbury Cathedral

John & Kay were at Peter's house for tea. Dave arrived & they went out to supper so that Kay could meet John's medical friends. (Dave works on a [illegible].) Nana took Simon to an Indian concert.

Peter took the children & Eve to a party at Gerald & Emily Stocock's house. 2 Chinese daughters, People played music; Gerald showed me the dress description ready for the BBC of Princess Di's dress.
Sunday Peter & Simon went to Quaker meeting. Nana & Eve took children to a street party. In the afternoon we went to Dinosaurs & Best horses in Concert. Then Peter & children went to a leaving party in a pub. In the evening Rocky came to Supper.
Monday At 7.15. Simon, Peter & Eve left on pedal bikes for Central London for a talk on the Bavagad Gita. It was an exciting ride. Army of cyclists on Embankment going v. fast. The Breakfast. Then India & China in BM. In the evening we went to a talk by John Grey.  May 22nd.
Tuesday Eve took children to school with Peter. We called in for a pub lunch with Judith & Rosemary on the way to Kennels. Then home
